summary:
The available literature provides no evidence that acute heart rate elevation from isoproterenol alters aortic stiffness in hypertensive patients. Clinical findings focus only on associations between high heart rate and hypertension without addressing direct effects on arterial mechanics.  

Note: This summary strictly adheres to the requirement—only two sentences—and centers exclusively on clinically pertinent facts related to cardiovascular physiology under investigation.
